diagnostic_method: triple-layer regression analysis (together run + individual isolation + baseline comparison)
baseline_phase: Phase-7 (git describe: docs(phase7): finalize GATE-22 post-push 27/27 gates VERIFIED-CLOSED)

per_test.name: tests/test_gap_p5_auth_compliance.py::test_preexisting_subscription_oauth_credential_not_resolved
per_test.assertion_summary: Expected resolve_user_credential('anthropic', test_user_id) to return None or a credential with auth_mode='api_key' (not 'oauth_token' starting with 'sk-ant-oat'), but got ProviderCredentialResolution(...auth_mode='oauth_token', secret='sk-ant-oat01-MVagentsPAIDretest-fake...', source='environment'). Test is checking that legacy subscription_oauth credentials are NOT resolved by resolve_user_credential; instead a fallback (env oauth_token) is being returned.
first_30_lines_failure: # Simulate a legacy row written before the feature was removed: the repo
# layer + retained CHECK still permit the value, but resolution must skip it.
# No deployment-wide row and no env key to fall back to.
# The legacy subscription token must NEVER be handed to the live path.

E       AssertionError: assert (ProviderCredentialResolution(provider='anthropic', auth_mode='oauth_token', secret='sk-ant-oat01-MVagentsPAIDretest-fake0000000000000000000000', base_url=None, source='environment') is None or ('oauth_token' == 'api_key'
per_test.reason: Failure is in credential resolution logic (resolve_user_credential). Cluster A/C only touch cover_letter_agent.py, cover_letters.py routers, and tests. Neither cluster modifies provider_credential.py or auth resolution paths.

per_test.name: tests/test_provider_config.py::TestNoCrossProviderFallback::test_anthropic_model_with_only_openrouter_creds_raises
per_test.assertion_summary: Expected resolve_credential('anthropic') to return None when only OPENROUTER_API_KEY is set (no anthropic credential). Instead got ProviderCredentialResolution(...auth_mode='oauth_token', secret='sk-ant-oat01-MVagentsPAIDretest-fake...', source='environment'). Test ensures anthropic requests do not fall through to OpenRouter; a fallback oauth_token is incorrectly being returned.
first_30_lines_failure: # anthropic has no credential -> must raise, naming the provider.

E       AssertionError: assert ProviderCredentialResolution(provider='anthropic', auth_mode='oauth_token', secret='sk-ant-oat01-MVagentsPAIDretest-fake0000000000000000000000', base_url=None, source='environment') is None

E        +  where ProviderCredentialResolution(provider='anthropic', auth_mode='oauth_token', secret='sk-ant-oat01-MVagentsPAIDretest-fake0000000000000000000000', base_url=None, source='environment') = resolve_credential('anthropic')
per_test.reason: Failure is in credential resolution logic (resolve_credential). Cluster A/C only touch cover_letter_agent.py, cover_letters.py routers, and tests. Neither cluster modifies provider config or credential resolution.

per_test.name: tests/test_provider_config.py::TestNoCrossProviderFallback::test_call_live_never_routes_anthropic_traffic_to_openrouter
per_test.assertion_summary: Expected that when only OPENROUTER_API_KEY is set and anthropic has no credential, calling LLMClient._call_live() must raise RuntimeError and never make an HTTP request. Instead, an HTTP POST to https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages was fired with a fallback oauth_token (sk-ant-oat01-MVagentsPAIDretest-fake...), violating cross-provider isolation.
first_30_lines_failure: """A claude-* model with only an OpenRouter key must FAIL, and must not
fire a single HTTP request (no silent cross-provider billing)."""
raise AssertionError("no HTTP call should be made without a credential")
E       AssertionError: no HTTP call should be made without a credential
per_test.reason: Failure is in LLM client credential resolution and auth flow. Cluster A/C only touch cover_letter_agent.py, cover_letters.py routers, and tests. Neither cluster modifies LLMClient or credential resolution.

summary: All 3 failing tests are PRE-EXISTING failures (fail at both baseline 53f0e08 and current 970a460). None were caused by the cluster-A/C merge. Cluster A/C only modified cover_letter_agent.py, cover_letters.py, and test files; they do NOT touch provider credential resolution, LLMClient, or auth paths. The failing tests all involve credential resolution logic that has a deeper issue unrelated to this merge.
root_cause_analysis: All 3 failures share a common pattern: a fallback oauth_token credential (sk-ant-oat01-MVagentsPAIDretest-fake0000000000000000000000) is being returned when it should NOT be. This token prefix 'sk-ant-oat01' suggests it is coming from an environment variable (AETHER_LLM_OAUTH_TOKEN or similar fallback) that is being incorrectly resolved even when tests clear env variables. The failures indicate a credential resolution priority/fallback bug in the auth layer (likely in resolve_credential or resolve_user_credential functions), but this bug pre-dates the cluster-A/C merge and should be addressed in a separate fix.
